title: WILDS PovertyMap Satellite and Socioeconomic Prediction Dataset

data_urls.access_notes: Use WILDS package and dataset documentation for official split metadata.

instructions: Use the WILDS package to load PovertyMap and official country/region folds.
Record country, region, latitude/longitude if available, time, image channels, and survey target.
Use sampled imagery or precomputed representations for iterative SPECTRA exploration.

scale.download_size: multi-GB satellite image dataset; use folds or embeddings for quick audits

recommended_axes.name: geographic_distance
recommended_axes.definition: Great-circle or region-level distance from training locations.

recommended_axes.name: environmental_image_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity in satellite image embeddings, spectral bands, land-cover, or nightlights features.

recommended_axes.name: socioeconomic_support
recommended_axes.definition: Distance from training survey target and covariate support; diagnostic unless only train labels are used for split construction.

useful_for: Testing whether country holdout corresponds to a continuous geographic or environmental novelty curve.
Non-biology SPECTRA demonstrations with real distribution shift.
leakage_risks: Nearby survey clusters can leak geographic context.
Target-derived poverty axes are post-hoc unless constructed from train-only data.
Country labels can conflate geography, policy, image coverage, and survey process.
